name: Backend Architecture
summary: A framework-adapter view of backend design that keeps policy, use cases, interfaces, and infrastructure distinct while managing change cost.

name: Clean Architecture
summary: Separate concerns into layers so business policy stays stable while frameworks, database details, and delivery mechanisms remain replaceable.

name: Dependency Rule
summary: Source-code dependencies should point inward toward policy layers; outward dependencies are corrected with abstractions and adapters.

name: Dependency Inversion
summary: Inner code depends on abstractions while outer adapters provide concrete implementations, keeping policy code free of framework and infrastructure detail.

name: Domain Layer
summary: Innermost policy layer for domain concepts and domain-facing abstractions; it should avoid framework, database, UI, and external-system dependencies.

name: Application Layer
summary: Use-case orchestration layer that coordinates domain policy and ports without owning transport or persistence mechanics.

name: Interface Layer
summary: Adapter layer that translates transport input and output into application calls and boundary DTOs.

name: Infrastructure Layer
summary: Outer layer for database, ORM, migrations, concrete repositories, external services, and operational integration details.

name: Repository Pattern
summary: Persistence boundary where application code speaks to a domain-facing abstraction and infrastructure supplies the concrete data access implementation.

name: Service Layer
summary: Application-level service or use-case coordinator that expresses a business flow without embedding framework routing or database details.

name: Use Case Boundary
summary: Boundary where a user or system intent enters application code and is coordinated through domain rules and ports.

name: DTO and Schema Boundary
summary: Boundary object shape that translates request or response data without letting transport schema concerns dominate domain policy.

name: Transaction Boundary
summary: Boundary where persistence consistency is coordinated while keeping database session mechanics from leaking through policy code.

name: Structure versus Behavior
summary: Behavior is what the system does; structure is how the system is organized to make behavior changes possible, cheap, or expensive.

name: Separate Structure and Behavior Changes
summary: Keep structural refactors and behavior changes distinguishable so review, rollback, and reasoning costs stay lower.

name: Coupling
summary: A cost relationship where changing one element forces knowledge or change in another element.

name: Decoupling
summary: Structural work is justified when reducing coupling lowers expected future change cost more than it costs now.

name: Cohesion
summary: Related elements should have a clear home so changes are localized, but new boundaries must still be worth their cost.

name: Change Cost
summary: Architecture decisions matter because they raise or lower the future cost of changing software.

name: Reversible Structure Change
summary: Prefer reversible structural steps under uncertainty because they preserve options and reduce regret.

name: Review and Rollback Cost
summary: The cost of understanding, approving, and reverting a change falls when structural work and behavior work stay separate.

name: Framework Leak Risk
summary: A boundary risk where framework APIs or concrete infrastructure details enter application or domain code and weaken dependency direction.

name: FastAPI Adapter
summary: Verified v1 framework adapter that maps FastAPI routing, dependency injection, response schemas, and SQLAlchemy integration onto backend architecture boundaries.

name: FastAPI Depends Layer Leak Risk
summary: FastAPI Depends is useful for wiring, but placing it inside application or domain services can make policy code depend on framework APIs.

name: FastAPI Router as Interface Adapter
summary: APIRouter and route functions belong at the interface boundary where HTTP input is adapted into application calls.

name: FastAPI Response Schema Boundary
summary: FastAPI response models shape boundary output and help keep sensitive or infrastructure fields out of public API responses.

name: FastAPI SQLAlchemy Infrastructure Adapter
summary: SQLAlchemy ORM, sessions, migrations, and concrete repositories are infrastructure details behind domain-facing ports.

name: Framework Adapter Extension Point
summary: Future adapters such as Django or NestJS should be added only with project evidence or public-safe source graphs, not by unverified analogy.

name: Spring Adapter
summary: Verified Spring adapter that maps Spring MVC, service, repository, JPA, OpenAPI, WebFlux, and Spring Security concepts onto backend architecture boundaries.

name: Spring Controller as Interface Adapter
summary: Spring MVC controllers and REST controllers adapt HTTP requests and responses into application calls and should not own business policy.

name: Spring Service as Application Coordinator
summary: Spring service components can act as application/use-case coordinators when they express business flows without transport or persistence mechanics.

name: Spring Repository Adapter
summary: Spring repository components form a persistence boundary; concrete data access should stay behind repository abstractions or application-facing ports.

name: Spring JPA Infrastructure Adapter
summary: JPA persistence, database configuration, and entity mapping are infrastructure concerns behind repository or persistence boundaries.

name: Spring OpenAPI Contract Boundary
summary: OpenAPI design-first specifications and generated interfaces shape API boundary contracts without becoming the owner of domain policy.

name: Spring WebFlux Reactive Boundary
summary: WebFlux, Reactive Streams, Mono, Flux, and R2DBC define reactive adapter and persistence boundaries for non-blocking API flows.

name: Spring Security Filter Boundary
summary: Spring Security filters, JWT handling, OAuth2 resource server behavior, roles, CORS, and CSRF belong at security/interface boundaries before stable identity or authorization data moves inward.

name: Spring Reactive Type Leak Risk
summary: Letting Mono, Flux, or reactive persistence details become domain model dependencies can couple policy code to WebFlux and Reactor choices.

name: Spring Security Context Leak Risk
summary: Leaking Spring Security context, filter, JWT, or OAuth2 mechanics into application/domain logic can blur authentication adapters with policy code.

name: API Contract Boundary
summary: API design turns system abstractions into stable external contracts through resources, actions, HTTP semantics, authentication, and authorization decisions.

name: Data Modeling Boundary
summary: Data modeling, schema, indexing, transactions, and storage choices are architecture decisions because persisted data outlives replaceable application code.

name: Data Layer Pattern
summary: A data layer encapsulates domain models, ORM behavior, unit-of-work style coordination, CQRS choices, and migrations behind a persistence boundary.

name: Event-Driven Boundary
summary: Queues and event flows decouple long-running or asynchronous work from request paths while adding explicit status, retry, scheduling, priority, and failure-handling responsibilities.

name: Monolith Microservice Tradeoff
summary: Monolith and microservice structures are options balanced by boundary clarity, team structure, change speed, operational capability, and communication cost.

name: Operational Readiness
summary: Backend architecture must account for configuration, dependencies, statelessness, deployment, runtime process shape, monitoring, and failure response as part of design.

name: Testing and TDD Feedback Loop
summary: Tests and TDD make architecture changes smaller, observable, and safer by providing fast feedback before broad structural change spreads.

name: Package Distribution Boundary
summary: Package management turns reusable code boundaries into versioned, deployable, and environment-isolated distribution units.

name: Observability Feedback Loop
summary: Logging, metrics, profiling, and debugging provide runtime evidence for narrowing failures, bottlenecks, capacity issues, and architecture improvement targets.

name: Continuous Architecture
summary: Architecture evolves through incremental, evidence-backed changes rather than one-time upfront design or broad rewrites.

name: DDD Strategic Design
summary: Strategic DDD locates valuable business problems, subdomains, language boundaries, and team ownership before implementation patterns are chosen.

name: DDD Subdomain Classification
summary: Core, supporting, and generic subdomains guide how much modeling investment, custom code, and ownership a backend area deserves.

name: Ubiquitous Language Boundary
summary: Ubiquitous language keeps conversations, tests, model names, and code aligned inside the boundary where business terms have coherent meaning.

name: Bounded Context Boundary
summary: A bounded context is a semantic and ownership boundary where a model and language stay internally consistent and translations are explicit at the edge.

name: Context Map Workflow
summary: A context map exposes bounded contexts, team ownership, upstream/downstream dependencies, and integration patterns so boundary risk becomes visible.

name: Anticorruption Layer Boundary
summary: An anticorruption layer translates external model concepts and data shapes so a local bounded context does not absorb upstream language or invariants.

name: Aggregate Consistency Boundary
summary: An aggregate groups related model objects around invariants and intentionally small transaction boundaries, usually accessed through repositories.

name: Domain Model Building Blocks
summary: Entities, value objects, aggregates, repositories, and domain events are tactical patterns for complex business behavior and invariants.

name: Simple Domain Pattern Choice
summary: Transaction script and active record are valid choices for simple or CRUD-heavy domains; rich domain models should be reserved for behavior and invariant complexity.

name: Event Sourcing and CQRS Boundary
summary: Event sourcing and CQRS split history, command decisions, and read models when business history or read/write divergence justifies operational complexity.

name: Event Storming Discovery
summary: Event storming discovers domain events, commands, policies, aggregates, actors, and bounded context candidates before committing to code structure.

name: Data Mesh Domain Ownership
summary: Data mesh extends domain boundary thinking to analytical data ownership, treating data products and language as domain responsibilities.

name: Modern Java Backend Style
summary: Modern Java idioms should clarify Spring/backend code through readable transformations, null boundaries, temporal values, and explicit async ownership without replacing architecture boundaries.

name: Java Optional Absence Boundary
summary: Optional is useful for lookup or API absence boundaries when DTO, persistence, and domain conventions remain explicit rather than treating Optional as a universal null replacement.

name: Java Stream Side Effect Discipline
summary: Stream pipelines improve backend readability only when transformations stay readable and business side effects or shared mutations remain explicit.

name: CompletableFuture Async Boundary
summary: CompletableFuture belongs at explicit async integration boundaries where executor ownership, timeout, cancellation, and error handling are part of the design.

name: Runtime Environment Boundary
summary: Backend architecture should treat dependency installation, ASGI/server runtime, local database containers, and framework startup as outer runtime concerns rather than business policy.

name: API Risk and Observability Boundary
summary: REST, GraphQL, and security mechanisms need a separate risk and observability boundary so protocol code, token handling, and runtime evidence are not treated as one concern.

name: Reversible Change Economics
summary: Architecture work should preserve options under uncertainty: small tidies, reversible structure changes, and volatility-aware option value reduce the cost of choosing too early.

name: Public-safe knowledge boundary
summary: 원문 내용을 개념과 관계로 활용하되 원문 표현과 예제 구조를 공개 그래프에 복제하지 않는 경계다.

name: Coverage matrix workflow
summary: chapter와 source slice별 추출 상태, gap, 누적 대체 위험을 추적해 얇은 샘플링과 과도한 요약을 함께 방지한다.

name: Source quality lane
summary: reviewed source와 lower-confidence source를 같은 강도로 쓰지 않고 normal/limited lane으로 나누는 원칙이다.

name: Curated topic index
summary: public chunks를 private chunks의 축약본이 아니라 검색과 학습의 topic index로 만드는 패턴이다.

name: Semantic query QA
summary: 대표 질문이 기대 node와 chunk를 함께 찾는지 확인해 그래프가 실제 질의에 쓰이는지 검증한다.

name: Canonical term registry
summary: 동일어, 유사어, framework별 구현 대응, 특화 관계를 구분해 cross-skill 용어 충돌을 관리한다.

name: Adapter evidence rule
summary: framework adapter로 승격하려면 public graph 근거와 adapter-eligible 정책이 함께 있어야 한다.

name: Independent source promotion
summary: 일반 backend 원칙은 최소 두 source skill의 독립 근거가 있거나 단일 framework adapter 근거가 명시되어야 한다.

name: Expression leakage stop gate
summary: 긴 동일 구문, 코드 예제 구조, 표/문제풀이 흐름이 public row에 나타나면 batch를 중단해야 한다.

name: Reviewed source normal lane
summary: 검수 완료 source는 넓은 coverage를 허용하되 verbatim gate와 query QA를 통과해야 한다.

name: Summary-only limited lane
summary: needs_review 또는 unreviewed source는 정확한 표현 기반 확장 대신 추상화된 개념과 관계만 제한적으로 반영한다.

name: Graph density sufficiency
summary: node 수만 늘리는 그래프는 추론성이 낮으므로 chunk 대비 node와 node 대비 edge 밀도를 함께 본다.

name: Source ref stability
summary: 공개 source ref는 stable source id와 review status를 남기고 절대 로컬 경로나 private chunk filename을 남기지 않는다.

name: Manual sample review
summary: 자동 lint가 잡지 못하는 표현 유사성은 변경 row 샘플을 private source와 대조하는 리뷰 루프로 보완한다.

name: Market substitute risk
summary: 여러 batch가 누적되어 원문의 목차와 핵심 논지를 대체할 정도로 촘촘해지는 위험을 별도 신호로 추적한다.

name: Heart of work risk
summary: 분량이 작아도 원문의 핵심 표현이나 독창적 설명 흐름을 옮기면 public-safe 기준에서 실패로 본다.

name: Cross-skill closeMatch
summary: 완전 동일하지는 않지만 검색상 함께 봐야 하는 개념은 same_as가 아니라 closeMatch로 연결한다.

name: Framework maps_to
summary: DDD나 architecture 개념이 Spring/FastAPI 구현 요소로 대응될 때는 동일성이 아니라 maps_to 관계로 기록한다.

name: Batch rollback unit
summary: 검증 실패 시 전체 작업이 아니라 해당 skill의 references batch를 되돌려 개선 루프를 작게 유지한다.

name: Public graph only meta skill
summary: backend meta skill은 raw OCR이나 private chunks가 아니라 source skills의 public graph ID만 참조한다.

name: Dependency Direction Principle Boundary
summary: Backend architecture에서 dependency direction principle의 boundary 관점은 여러 source skill의 public graph를 연결해 책임과 검증 기준을 정한다.
